steps.title: Treat the expression as one unit
steps.content:
Let the repeated expression $4-6x$ stay grouped. Subtract $4(4-6x)$ from both sides.
$$5(4-6x)+2=4(4-6x)+12$$
$$(4-6x)+2=12$$

steps.title: Solve for the grouped value
steps.content: Subtract $2$ from both sides. $$4-6x=10$$ The answer is D.
